info.title: Dot TimeWork
info.description:
DotTimeWork is a simple time tracking commandline tool that helps you keep track of your working time and tasks.
It works by executing single commands one after another, similar to the DotNet CLI.

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: A tool to help you track your working time and manage your projects.

commands.name: CreateProject
commands.description: Creates Project Config file. This will create a new project config file in the current directory. The config file will be used to store the project settings and tasks.

commands.name: Details
commands.description: Shows details of a specific Task.

commands.name: Developer
commands.description: Creates Developer Config file. This will create a new developer config file in the current directory. The config file will be used to store the developer settings.

commands.name: List
commands.description: Lists all tasks. This will list all tasks in the current project. The tasks will be listed in the order they were created.

commands.name: Start
commands.description: Can start / mark TimeStamp when a new Task was started working on.

commands.name: Work
commands.description: Active Time Tracking for a specific Task - Focus Work

commands.name: Comment
commands.description: Add a comment to the current task. This will add a comment to the current task. The comment will be used to store the task details and notes.

commands.name: Report
commands.description: Generate a report of all active and completed Tasks

commands.name: csv
commands.description: Generate a CSV report of all active and completed Tasks

commands.name: html
commands.description: Generate a HTML report of all active and completed Tasks
